element_introduction_order.mastery_context: staple early game enemy type establishing baseline combat
main antagonistic force for Chapter 1, coup forces
main enemy kingdom forces throughout mid-to-late game
often controlled by Duma Faithful dark sorcerer cult
Alm promotes fighter→hero, Celica priestess→princess; non-protagonist units gain promotion options
learned through leveling, each cast consumes caster health
freely navigable map opening up as story progresses, towns for recruitment and trading
two opposing armies with separate campaigns that converge at Zofia Castle, then diverge again

typical_tension_triggers: map introduction of new enemy faction
health-consuming magic use creating resource tension
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: alternates between overworld exploration/town interaction (relief) and grid-based tactical battles (tension); dual-protagonist design creates narrative tension through separation and eventual convergence

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: overworld areas opening progressively as story advances; towns accessible for optional recruitment and trading

difficulty_curve.early_game: Bandits and Desaix's forces in Chapter 1, establishing baseline tactical challenge
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Pirates in Chapter 2 transitioning to seaborne encounters; Grieth's desert forces in Chapter 3; introduction of Rigel Army proper
difficulty_curve.late_game: Rigel Empire forces intensify; monster units controlled by Duma Faithful dark sorcerers; final infiltration of Duma Temple
notable_spikes: Transition from Zofia civil war to Rigel invasion

notable_level_patterns.description: Game features Alm and Celica leading separate armies through distinct campaigns. They meet at Zofia Castle mid-game for narrative purpose, then separate again. This creates rhythm of isolation-reunification-isolation tension across chapters.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Zofia Castle meeting point; subsequent divergence into Rigel invasion (Alm) vs. Mila pilgrimage (Celica)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each chapter introduces new primary antagonistic force (Desaix→Grieth→Rigel) with associated generic unit types. Progression through continent with multiple active factions creates escalating complexity.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Chapter 1 (Desaix coup), Chapter 2 (Pirates/seaborne), Chapter 3 (Grieth eastern desert)

notable_level_patterns.description: Freely navigable overworld with progressive area unlocking tied to story advancement. Towns serve as safe havens for recruitment, trading, and exposition between battle phases.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Persistent between all chapters; overworld accessibility expands as campaign progresses

notable_level_patterns.description: Character progression tied to level thresholds enabling class switching. Promotion events create power scaling opportunities and tactical role diversification mid-campaign.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Applied across all recruitment windows; protagonist promotions (Alm/Celica) are narrative-story-gated
